channel.title: DevFocus - 开发者聚焦
channel.description: 每日自动整理的开发者资讯：AI 热榜、GitHub 趋势、技术新闻

item.title: [github_trending] OpenCut-app/OpenCut
item.description: OpenCut is an open-source video editor designed as a free alternative to CapCut. It offers powerful editing features without any subscription or watermark, making it ideal for content creators who want full control over their tools. The project is cross-platform and community-driven, ensuring transparency and continuous improvement. For anyone tired of paid video editors or restrictive platforms, OpenCut provides a flexible and privacy-respecting solution.

item.title: [github_trending] ocornut/imgui
item.description: Dear ImGui is a bloat-free graphical user interface library for C++ with minimal dependencies, offering a seamless immediate mode GUI experience. It is especially popular among game developers and tool builders who need quick, interactive panels for debugging or real-time data visualization without the overhead of traditional UI frameworks. Its focus on simplicity and performance makes it a go-to choice for indie games and embedded tools.

item.title: [github_trending] chrislgarry/Apollo-11
item.description: The original Apollo Guidance Computer source code was uploaded to GitHub, sparking a wave of tech nostalgia. This code represents the core logic behind the first moon landing navigation system, including both the command and lunar modules. For history enthusiasts and programmers, it offers a unique glimpse into how early engineers achieved spaceflight computing with minimal instructions and core memory. It's not just a historical artifact but a showcase of classic embedded system design.

item.title: [github_trending] koala73/worldmonitor
item.description: koala73/worldmonitor is an open source real-time global intelligence dashboard that leverages AI to aggregate news, monitor geopolitical shifts, and track infrastructure status in a single situational awareness interface. It‘s designed for journalists, analysts, and emergency responders who need to quickly grasp complex global events and make informed decisions from scattered data.

item.title: [github_trending] asgeirtj/system_prompts_leaks
item.description: This GitHub repository compiles leaked system prompts from popular AI models like Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Opus 4.8, as well as OpenAI's ChatGPT 5.5 Thinking and GPT 5.5 Instant. By exposing the hidden instructions that shape how these models behave, it offers rare insight into prompt engineering, safety guardrails, and role-setting techniques. AI researchers and developers can learn how top models are orchestrated, but the leak also raises important questions about access control and inte

item.title: [github_trending] Panniantong/Agent-Reach
item.description: Agent-Reach is a CLI tool that gives AI agents the ability to 'see' the entire internet. It enables reading and searching across major platforms like Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, and Xiaohongshu — all without any API fees. The biggest pain point for AI agent developers is getting real-time, cross-platform data; Agent-Reach solves this with a single command. Whether you're building a social media analyzer, a tech trend tracker, or a content aggregator, you can now equip your AI wit

item.title: [github_trending] usestrix/strix
item.description: Strix is an open-source AI-powered penetration testing tool designed to automatically identify and fix vulnerabilities in applications. By simulating cyber attacks with artificial intelligence, it helps developers catch security flaws early in the development cycle, significantly reducing manual effort and cost. This tool is particularly valuable for startups and individual developers who lack dedicated security teams, offering a practical way to strengthen app security without complex infrastru

item.title: [github_trending] calesthio/OpenMontage
item.description: OpenMontage is the world's first open-source, agentic video production system, featuring 12 pipelines, 52 tools, and over 500 agent skills. It transforms your AI coding assistant into a full-fledged video production studio, automating tasks like scriptwriting, editing, voiceover, and effects. This solves the complexity and high cost of traditional video creation, enabling anyone to produce professional videos using natural language commands. Content creators, marketers, and developers benefit fr

item.title: [github_trending] DioxusLabs/dioxus
item.description: Dioxus is a full-stack app framework written in Rust, aiming to replace React for desktop, web, and mobile development. It leverages Rust's safety and performance while offering a React-like declarative UI writing experience. Most notably, it allows the same codebase to run on multiple platforms including native desktop and mobile apps, making it a powerful tool for Rust developers seeking cross-platform consistency and high performance.

item.title: [github_trending] AstrBotDevs/AstrBot
item.description: AstrBot is an AI agent assistant and development framework that integrates numerous IM platforms, large language models, plugins, and AI features, positioning itself as an alternative to openclaw. It enables developers to quickly build cross-platform AI assistants with flexible plugin extensions and multi-model switching. For teams looking to deploy intelligent chatbots or automated workflows on WeChat, Discord, and other channels, it provides an out-of-the-box efficient solution.

item.title: [36kr] 谷歌密谋「焊死」Gemini，神秘芯片曝光，能效碾压TPU十倍
item.description: Google is reportedly secretly developing a custom AI chip specifically optimized for its Gemini model, claiming energy efficiency ten times that of current TPUs. This mysterious chip aims to tightly couple hardware and software with Gemini, significantly reducing inference costs and boosting performance. If true, this could reshape the AI chip landscape, especially for enterprises deploying Gemini at scale, offering lower operational costs and stronger competitiveness.

item.title: [36kr] 8点1氪丨怡颗莓回应“检出致癌物”；美团否认上线“本科专送”“硕士优送”等配送服务；美方喊话三星电子、SK海力士分享超额利润
item.description: Today's hot topics: Berry & Berry responded to claims of detecting carcinogens in their products, denying any safety issues. Meituan denied launching delivery services labeled as 'Bachelor's Degree Exclusive' or 'Master's Degree Premium', calling them rumors. The U.S. government urged Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix to share excess profits from chip market fluctuations. These developments touch on food safety, labor discrimination, and international trade dynamics, each sparking widespread atte

item.title: [36kr] 黄仁勋的“达链”闭环了
item.description: Jensen Huang's 'DA Chain' has closed the loop, referring to NVIDIA's achievement of a full-stack ecosystem from chip design to AI data centers. Previously reliant on external foundries and software adaptation, NVIDIA now integrates its own CPUs, GPUs, networking, and software platform into a seamless supply chain. This gives data center customers more efficient deployment and lower total cost of ownership, while making it harder for competitors to replicate such integrated advantages.

item.title: [36kr] 沿用DeepSeek架构，美国大模型开始抄中国作业
item.description: American large language models are now adopting DeepSeek's architecture, marking the first time a Chinese AI technical approach has been emulated by mainstream overseas teams. DeepSeek's innovations in sparse attention and MoE optimization significantly cut training costs while maintaining performance. The fact that US teams are directly replicating this design shows that China's open-source AI innovation has gained international recognition, shifting the competition from raw compute power to ar

item.title: [36kr] 磁吸背屏扎堆来了：影石海信卖功能，OPPO荣耀做情绪搭子
item.description: Magnetic back-screen phones are suddenly all the rage, but each brand takes a different approach. Insta360 and Hisense focus on practicality—using the back screen for information display or camera assistance. OPPO and Honor, on the other hand, treat it as an 'emotional companion' that shows animated emojis, weather, or custom animations. This divergence shows manufacturers are exploring how a second screen can create social value and user stickiness beyond sheer hardware gimmicks.

item.title: [36kr] 欧林生物：重庆啤酒的痛，市场还没忘记
item.description: Olym Bio has caught market attention recently, but investors haven't forgotten 'Chongqing Beer's pain'. That company saw its stock crash after a hepatitis B vaccine failure. Now Olym Bio is also betting big on innovative vaccines, and the market worries history might repeat. Despite Olym's strengths in tetanus vaccines, the uncertainty around its core pipeline keeps shareholders uneasy—a cautionary tale about single-product risk in biotech investing.

item.title: [36kr] 让张益唐苦熬7年的雅可比猜想，Fable 5竟一夜推翻证伪
item.description: The Jacobian Conjecture, which tormented mathematician Yitang Zhang for seven years, was overturned and falsified overnight by an AI tool called Fable 5. Using automated reasoning and counterexample search, Fable 5 found that the conjecture fails under certain conditions, ending years of academic debate. The mathematics community is both shocked and thrilled—AI not only assists in proofs but can now outright debunk classic conjectures, signaling that computational tools will play an even more ag

item.title: [36kr] 日本，缘何成为中国新茶饮“滑铁卢”？
item.description: Chinese new-style tea brands expanded into Japan with high hopes, only to face a Waterloo. Conservative consumer habits, fierce competition from established local drinks, and a rejection of premium pricing have made profitability elusive. This serves as a cautionary tale about the critical need for cultural adaptation and localization strategies when Chinese consumer brands venture overseas.

item.title: [36kr] 数码圈「跳水王」来了，手机电脑疯涨，相机价格却崩了
item.description: The electronics market is seeing a peculiar divergence: smartphones and PCs are skyrocketing in price due to chip shortages and rising component costs, while cameras are crashing in value as smartphone photography eats their lunch. For consumers, it's a great time to buy a camera bargain but a painful one for phones or laptops. This price inversion reflects deep shifts in technology cycles and consumer behavior.

item.title: [36kr] 谷歌Gemini 4开跑了，一夜三连发
item.description: Google dropped the Gemini 4 family overnight, releasing three models — Ultra, Pro, and Nano — spanning cloud to on-device. These models boast major leaps in reasoning and multimodal capabilities, and are already being integrated into Google Search and Workspace. This means AI assistants are about to get smarter and more real-time, and developers have a new foundation for powerful apps. The AI race just shifted gears.

item.title: [infoq] 专访孙元浩：乘坐星环号，把数据库“移民火星”的人
item.description: In this interview, Sun Yuanhao, founder of Transwarp, discusses his vision of 'migrating databases to Mars' — a metaphor for moving legacy database systems to modern, cloud-native architectures. He explains Transwarp's innovations in distributed databases and data cloud platforms, and how they help enterprises smoothly transition their data infrastructure. The piece highlights China's growing ambition in database technology and offers insights into how domestic companies can leapfrog traditional

item.title: [infoq] 业务研发中的 Loop Engineering：基于事前-事中-事后架构的实践｜AICon深圳
item.description: Loop Engineering introduces a novel approach to business development by structuring fault handling into three phases: before (prevention), during (response), and after (review). By embedding automated detection, isolation, and recovery into each stage, it dramatically reduces the impact of failures on operations. This architecture is ideal for internet teams chasing high availability and fast iteration, turning reliability from a manual firefight into an engineering discipline.

item.title: [infoq] 无问芯穹夏立雪：数字世界与物理世界的所有AI生产力运行，都需要 Agentic Infra
item.description: Infinite Foundry puts forward the concept of Agentic Infra, arguing that all AI productivity spanning the digital and physical worlds requires such an intelligent infrastructure. It unifies compute orchestration, data management, and agent coordination, enabling robots, autonomous driving, and industrial AI to collaborate efficiently. This isn't just a tech stack upgrade — it's the foundational layer that moves AI from isolated applications to full-scenario automation.

item.title: [infoq] 国产GPU分水岭时刻：当别家还在卷参数，摩尔线程早已训练出了世界模型
item.description: Moore Threads has trained a world model using its domestic GPU, marking a watershed moment for the Chinese GPU industry. While competitors are still competing on hardware specifications, Moore Threads has demonstrated real-world AI training capability. This breakthrough shows that domestic GPUs can handle advanced AI workloads, which is critical for building a self-sufficient AI ecosystem and reducing dependence on foreign chips.

item.title: [infoq] 神州数码发布企业AI流程系统与智算产品，尝试打通应用和算力两端
item.description: Digital China has launched an enterprise AI workflow system and intelligent computing products, aiming to connect AI applications with computing power resources. The solution simplifies AI deployment and optimizes resource utilization, lowering the technical barriers and costs for enterprises to adopt AI. This move accelerates digital and intelligent transformation for businesses of all sizes.

item.title: [infoq] 亚马逊云科技概要介绍了某客户如何将 Lambda 函数扩展至 100 万个
item.description: AWS outlined how a customer scaled its Lambda functions to 1 million concurrent executions. The case study demonstrates the extreme elasticity of serverless computing and provides practical insights into handling massive workloads. It serves as a valuable reference for developers building highly scalable applications, showing that Lambda can handle unprecedented traffic spikes while maintaining performance and cost efficiency.

item.title: [infoq] 开源如何促进平台构建过程中的协作
item.description: This article explores how open source fosters collaboration in platform engineering. By sharing components, best practices, and code, teams can avoid reinventing the wheel and build platforms faster. Open source enables cross-team cooperation and leverages community contributions to improve systems. This approach is especially beneficial for platform engineers and DevOps teams aiming to accelerate development and innovate more efficiently.

item.title: [infoq] OpenAI 将崩溃调试视为流行病学研究，修复了存在 18 之久年的 GNU libunwind 漏洞
item.description: OpenAI treated a crash debugging process like an epidemiological investigation, ultimately identifying and fixing an 18-year-old bug in GNU libunwind. This creative approach combined system debugging with data analysis, offering a fascinating lesson for software developers. The fix benefits the entire open source community by addressing a long-standing vulnerability in a widely used library, highlighting the value of rigorous debugging methods and cross-team collaboration.

item.title: [infoq] 龙虾之父一条推文，Loop 时代终结？
item.description: A single tweet from the 'father of Lobster' has sent shockwaves through the tech community, hinting at the possible end of the Loop framework. Loop, once a darling of microservices architecture, enjoyed widespread adoption, but its creator's public remarks now leave developers uncertain. This incident underscores the risks of relying too heavily on a single visionary in open-source projects and prompts teams to rethink their framework dependencies and long-term viability.

item.title: [infoq] Java 近期新闻：TornadoVM 5、JHipster、Google ADK、OmniFish Build of Payara、Vidocq 发布
item.description: This Java news roundup covers key updates: TornadoVM 5 improves GPU acceleration for Java; JHipster delivers a new release to streamline microservice scaffolding; Google ADK launches to assist Android development; OmniFish introduces a community build of Payara; and Vidocq tool goes official. These releases collectively boost performance, developer productivity, and ecosystem expansion, making them essential reads for Java developers tracking the latest tooling and runtime advances.

item.title: [hackernews] Show HN: I replaced a $120k bowling center system with $1,600 in ESP32s
item.description: A developer replaced a $120,000 bowling center management system with just $1,600 worth of ESP32 microcontrollers. The project demonstrates how low-cost hardware and clever software design can handle core functions like scoring, sensor integration, and user management. For small bowling alley operators or embedded systems enthusiasts, this is an inspiring example of how to achieve professional-grade automation on a shoestring budget.

item.title: [hackernews] China’s open-weights AI strategy is winning
item.description: This analysis argues that China's strategy of releasing open-weight AI models is proving to be a winning approach against the more closed models preferred by many US companies. By openly sharing weights of powerful large language models, Chinese firms like Alibaba and Baidu attract global developers who can fine-tune and deploy these models freely. This accelerates innovation and adoption, lowers barriers for small businesses and researchers, and could shift the balance of AI power. The piece of

item.title: [hackernews] Qwen 3.8
item.description: Qwen 3.8 is the latest open-source large language model from Alibaba's Qwen family, boasting over 38 billion parameters. It delivers significant improvements in mathematical reasoning, code generation, and multilingual comprehension, with faster inference speed and stronger support for both Chinese and English. Because it remains fully open-source, developers, researchers, and enterprises can deploy it locally or fine-tune it for customized tasks. This makes Qwen 3.8 a compelling choice for buil

item.title: [hackernews] Who's afraid of Chinese models?
item.description: A Hacker News discussion explores whether Chinese AI models should be feared. As models like DeepSeek gain international traction, concerns about data privacy and security emerge. However, some argue this is just normal tech competition. The piece suggests that overblown fear is unwarranted, urging a fact-based risk assessment instead of emotional reactions, while highlighting opportunities from open-source collaboration and global innovation.

item.title: [producthunt] OpenSEO
item.description: OpenSEO is an open-source SEO toolkit marketed as a free alternative to Ahrefs. It includes website crawling, keyword research, backlink analysis, and rank tracking, all running locally so no subscription or third-party service is needed. This empowers small business owners, independent bloggers, and budget-conscious marketers to access professional-level SEO insights at zero cost, helping them discover content opportunities, monitor competitors, and boost organic traffic. Because the community

item.title: [hackernews] Airport Simulator
item.description: Airport Simulator is a detailed simulation tool that puts you in charge of a busy airport, managing everything from flight scheduling and runway allocation to gate assignments and baggage handling. More than just a game, it serves as an educational platform for aviation management, offering realistic physics and real-time decision-making challenges. Users get an immersive look into the coordination and pressure behind airport operations. It's a valuable resource for aviation enthusiasts, student

item.title: [hackernews] Claude Fable produced a counterexample to the Jacobian Conjecture
item.description: Claude, under a framework called Fable, has reportedly produced a counterexample to the Jacobian Conjecture, a long-standing open problem in algebraic geometry. The conjecture asserts that a polynomial map with non-zero constant Jacobian is invertible, and despite many partial proofs, no general counterexample had been found. Claude's construction, if validated, would be a significant mathematical breakthrough. This news also highlights how advanced AI models can contribute to frontier research

item.title: [hackernews] Hacker wipes Romania's land registry database
item.description: A hacker infiltrated Romania's national land registry system and wiped the entire database, causing the loss of millions of land ownership records. The attack disrupted government operations and plunged real estate transactions and title verification into chaos. Security experts warn that such destructive attacks on critical infrastructure are becoming more common, highlighting the urgent need for stronger cybersecurity measures worldwide.

item.title: [hackernews] OpenAI and Hugging Face address security incident during model evaluation
item.description: OpenAI and Hugging Face disclosed a security incident that occurred during a model evaluation, involving potential data exposure risks. Both companies responded quickly, patching the vulnerability and reinforcing security protocols in the evaluation pipeline. This incident highlights that AI model evaluation stages are not immune to attacks, especially in collaborative and open-testing environments, underscoring the need for rigorous security review processes to protect sensitive data.

item.title: [hackernews] Kimi Work
item.description: Kimi Work is a productivity platform designed for workplace teams, combining task management, calendar sync, and instant messaging to reduce information silos. Its standout feature is intelligent priority sorting and automated workflows, making it ideal for remote teams or cross-department collaboration scenarios.

item.title: [hackernews] Jelly UI: Soft-body physics for native HTML form controls
item.description: Jelly UI is an open-source library that adds soft-body physics to native HTML form controls, making inputs and buttons exhibit elastic deformation and natural bounce on interaction. It enhances the fun and feedback of form actions, allowing developers to create Apple Vision Pro-style dynamic interfaces without complex code.

item.title: [producthunt] Fuzzy AI
item.description: Fuzzy AI is a prospect warming tool that uses artificial intelligence to engage potential customers in natural conversations before you reach out. It solves the problem of cold outreach by building interest and trust gradually, making the first touchpoint more effective. B2B sales teams benefit most, as Fuzzy AI transforms awkward cold emails into warm, welcomed interactions, significantly boosting reply rates and conversions.

item.title: [producthunt] Loova Ads Studio
item.description: Loova Ads Studio is an AI-powered ad creation tool that goes beyond aesthetics to optimize for conversion. It automatically tests different copy, images, and audience segments, then recommends the highest-performing combination. Small businesses and marketers benefit most, as Loova acts like a dedicated ad optimizer without requiring design expertise, helping them achieve better ROI with less effort.

item.title: [producthunt] Nautis
item.description: Nautis is an AI-native operating system designed specifically for founders, integrating project management, CRM, financial tracking, and more. Its AI generates insights and suggests next steps automatically, freeing founders from administrative overhead so they can focus on strategy. Early-stage startup teams benefit most, as Nautis becomes the single brain that keeps everything aligned and actionable.

item.title: [producthunt] Lev8
item.description: Lev8 is a lead generation and contact discovery tool that helps sales, recruitment, and marketing professionals precisely find target people and quickly obtain their contact info and background. It integrates multi-channel data profiling to reduce manual search time, making it especially useful for B2B prospecting or executive recruiting.

item.title: [producthunt] Replay QA
item.description: Replay QA is an intelligent quality monitoring tool that records user session replays and automatically analyzes them for anomalies, catching bugs before users report them. It shifts the approach from reactive firefighting to proactive prevention, reducing the impact of issues on customers. Development and QA teams benefit most, as Replay QA acts like a round-the-clock sentinel, ensuring problems are spotted and fixed early.

item.title: [producthunt] Spycost
item.description: Spycost is a price-tracking and discount verification tool that helps shoppers determine whether a sale is genuine. By simply pasting a product URL, it displays the item's historical price chart, showing the lowest, average, and current prices, and flags inflated original prices and fake discount percentages. For frequent online shoppers who often fall for limited-time deals, Spycost provides transparency before checkout, revealing the true value of a product and preventing impulse buys driven b

item.title: [producthunt] CartAI
item.description: CartAI is an AI agent that automates the checkout process on e-commerce sites, handling address entry, payment selection, and more. It solves the pain point of repetitive manual form filling, making it ideal for frequent online shoppers or those racing for limited-edition items, as well as developers testing checkout flows.

item.title: [producthunt] ditto.site
item.description: ditto.site is a free, open-source tool that clones any website into clean, editable HTML and CSS code. It captures the page structure with one click and strips out redundant scripts, making it handy for front-end developers to obtain reference layouts or for designers to extract inspiration. It's fully open source and requires no sign-up.

item.title: [producthunt] Skippr AI
item.description: Skippr AI is a live AI employee that lives inside your product, serving every user in real time. It solves the problem of slow customer support and high labor costs by turning your product itself into a conversational assistant. Whether answering questions, guiding users, or collecting feedback, Skippr interacts naturally like a real team member. For SaaS products, e-commerce platforms, or any app requiring user guidance and support, it dramatically improves user experience and operational effic

item.title: [juejin] Codex 穷鬼大救星
item.description: This article introduces a budget-friendly AI coding workflow called "Poor Guy Workflow," aimed at helping developers with limited funds leverage tools like Codex to complete projects at low cost. The author humorously dubs it an "AI shady contractor team," emphasizing that it allows coders on a tight budget to enjoy AI efficiency boosts without expensive subscriptions. It's especially practical for indie developers and small teams.

item.title: [juejin] 小程序上线半年我赚了多少钱？
item.description: An indie developer shared the real revenue and operational data of his expense tracking mini-program called 'Fuzhu' over six months since launch. He iterated from v1.0.0 to v1.6.1, documenting development logs, user growth, and profitability. This article demonstrates the possibility of building a product in spare time, offering a complete postmortem from coding to business. It’s especially useful for programmers exploring side projects.

item.title: [juejin] Cursor 转 Codex 大半个月，聊聊我的真实感受
item.description: After switching from Cursor to Amazon Codex for over half a month, a developer shared their real experience of the fundamental differences. While Cursor first impressed them with AI-assisted coding, Codex felt like a true super agent that can independently execute tasks, not just an assistant. The author believes Codex represents the next phase of AI programming—evolving from conversational completion to proactive agency. This comparison is invaluable for developers seeking the cutting edge in A

item.title: [juejin] 写给年轻程序员的几点建议-2
item.description: The author offers advice for young programmers in the post-internet-bonus era, emphasizing the need to develop business acumen and writing skills. Given China's huge population, successfully selling a product or writing a viral WeChat article can bring significant wealth. The article argues that market insight and content communication are as crucial as technical skills, making it relevant for early-career programmers looking to diversify their income streams.

item.title: [juejin] 最近公司烧token比招员工还贵，老板干懵了...
item.description: This isn't a joke—it's a real dilemma many companies are facing. After wholeheartedly adopting AI, their token usage costs have actually surpassed the expense of hiring new employees. Uber's CTO revealed that over 90% of their engineers are using AI services heavily. Bosses are staring at the bills in shock: the technology that was supposed to reduce costs and boost efficiency has instead become a new money-burning black hole.

item.title: [juejin] 停更的这一年，All In AI、陪媳妇生娃、还考了个研
item.description: A Juejin author returns after a year-long hiatus, recounting a whirlwind period of diving headfirst into AI, supporting his wife through childbirth, and even squeezing in graduate school exams. Through first-person narration, he shares how he balanced career, family, and personal growth, offering a relatable and inspiring tale for developers navigating life’s major milestones while striving to keep improving.

item.title: [juejin] 2026编程圈很火的10个Skills
item.description: The 10 hottest Skills in programming circles for 2026 refer not to developers' own abilities but to standardized workflow modules for AI coding assistants. These skill packages transform AI from random code generation to structured delivery, covering code review, test generation, documentation, and more. For teams and individual developers exploring AI-assisted programming, mastering these skills is becoming essential for boosting productivity and code quality.

item.title: [juejin] Kimi k3是真碉，但太不经用，也有拉跨点！
item.description: The author tested Kimi's K3 model and found it genuinely powerful, but the critical issue is its extremely limited quota. A week's worth of quota was exhausted in just one day, allowing only five test page generations. Among the examples, some results were impressive while others were disappointing or even poor. The author stresses this is an unbiased review, highlighting that K3, despite its strength, suffers from severe availability constraints that undermine its practical use.

item.title: [juejin] Kimi 停售，GLM限购，国内用户还是难啊！
item.description: Just yesterday the author was busy testing Kimi K3, and today news broke that it has been discontinued for sale. K3 became so popular globally that sales were halted, similar to the earlier GLM 5.2 which also remained under purchase restrictions due to high demand. This situation highlights the difficulty domestic users face in accessing hot AI models—it's not just foreign models that are hard to get, but now even domestic ones are equally elusive.

item.title: [zhihu] 网友发现很多 KTV 都没有 MV 了，全是奇怪的 AI 画面，为什么会这样？你有遇到这种情况吗？
item.description: A netizen noticed that many KTV rooms now play bizarre AI-generated visuals instead of traditional music videos, sparking discussions about why this change is happening and how it affects the singing experience. The shift likely relates to copyright costs, content production efficiency, and the digital transformation trend in the KTV industry. This phenomenon raises questions about the balance between technology convenience and user experience in entertainment venues.

item.title: [zhihu] 如何看待申真谞二比一战胜围棋人工智能「KataGo」？
item.description: Top Go player Shin Jin-seo defeated the Go AI KataGo with a 2–1 score, sparking heated discussion. While KataGo is not as dominant as AlphaGo, a human victory under handicap or specific conditions still demonstrates the subtle interplay between human intuition and AI calculation. This outcome raises questions about whether humans can still find breakthroughs against AI or if it reveals limitations in current Go AI systems, offering valuable insights for the ongoing human-AI competition.

item.title: [zhihu] Kimi 新模型 K3 跑分力压 ClaudeFable5 登顶，这将对大模型市场格局产生哪些影响？
item.description: Kimi's new model K3 has topped the benchmark rankings, surpassing Claude Fable 5, which is set to significantly impact the landscape of large language models. This achievement signals that domestic models have reached a new level of technical prowess, potentially reshaping industry competition and pushing other developers to accelerate their iterations. However, whether the benchmark success will translate into real-world application advantages remains to be seen, as the gap between rankings and

item.title: [zhihu] 情侣用 AI 判断菌子毒性采了 10 余斤，被本地小孩哥筛选只剩 2 斤，AI 和小孩哥谁的判断更准？为啥会有不同？
item.description: A couple used AI to identify poisonous mushrooms and proudly collected over ten pounds, only to have a local child winnow them down to just two pounds of safe-to-eat ones. This charming incident highlights a critical gap: AI mushrooms identification relies on image databases and struggles with visual variants, while the child's knowledge comes from generations of hands-on local wisdom about smell, texture, and habitat. For outdoor enthusiasts and AI-reliant novices, the takeaway is clear—AI is a

item.title: [zhihu] 怎么看谷歌发布了 Gemini 3.6 flash 和 3.5 lite，但是 pro 又缺席？
item.description: Google quietly launched two new Gemini models: 3.6 Flash, optimized for fast inference and low cost, and 3.5 Lite, a lightweight variant for edge devices. But the Pro model is again missing, raising eyebrows. Strategically, Google seems to be segmenting its lineup—Flash targets real-time chat and high-frequency tasks, while Lite fits mobile and IoT. Pro, the flagship, may still be in refinement or awaiting a bigger launch window. For developers, this means practical tools for prototyping are her

item.title: [zhihu] Claude Fable 5 正式退出 Pro 订阅，但是保留了 Max ，这是为什么？
item.description: Anthropic has quietly removed Claude Fable 5 from its Pro subscription, making it exclusive to the pricier Max plan. Behind this move is simple economics: Fable 5, a model specialized for creative writing, is computationally expensive to run, and the $20/month Pro tier couldn't sustain it profitably. By keeping it in Max, Anthropic ensures the model's quality while aligning with high-paying users' expectations. For regular Pro subscribers, it's a loss of a unique writer's assistant, but core Cla

item.title: [zhihu] Linux 中「一切皆文件」是什么意思？
item.description: In Linux, 'everything is a file' is a core philosophy that treats almost any system resource—devices, processes, sockets, even kernel data—as if they were regular files. This means you can open a keyboard, read from a microphone, or write to a hard drive using the same read/write/close operations you'd use on a text file. It massively simplifies programming: common tools like cat, grep, or redirection work on all sorts of resources. For beginners, grasping this unlocks the true power of the comm

item.title: 📅 历史归档
item.description: 查看往日精选与趋势变化
